title: Workspace Identity / Membership / Ownership Implementation Plan
decision_rationale: The implementation plan is complete, internally consistent, and safe to proceed. The current workspace_id = NULL posture is explicitly documented and tracked since GL-144. A structured phased plan is required before workspace enforcement can be safely implemented without risking audit integrity, tenant isolation regression, or demo-flow breakage. No production-readiness tier changes. No real data enabled. No migrations added. No backend/src changes. Post-enforcement go/no-go gate (GL-231) required before any NO-GO tier reassessment.

current_tenant_workspace_state_summary.description: tenant_id is server-derived and enforced on all business resource routes. workspace_id column exists on all resource tables (added by migration 0010) but is NULL for all records. No workspaces or workspace_members tables exist. No workspace membership verification in any route handler. No cross-workspace denial guards. Audit events carry workspace_id = null throughout. Operator model is tenant-aware but not workspace-aware.

current_workspace_trust_gaps.gap: workspace_id is nullable/unset on all resources — no workspace enforcement
No workspaces entity — a workspace cannot be created or looked up
No workspace_members entity — no operator/workspace association
No workspace-scoped authorization in server routes
No workspace ownership model on resources
Audit events carry workspace_id = null — no workspace audit trail
No workspace migration/backfill plan for existing null records

workspace_identity_model.description: A workspace is a named, scoped operating context within a tenant with a stable UUID, human-readable name, tenant_id (immutable), owner_operator_id, active boolean, and created_at timestamp.
workspace_identity_model.trust_model: workspace_id is server-assigned. Clients cannot supply or override workspace_id on any resource write. workspace_id is derived from the authenticated operator context, not from request body parameters.
workspace_identity_model.planned_schema_entity: workspaces table: id, tenant_id, name, description, owner_id, active, created_at, updated_at, UNIQUE(tenant_id, name)

membership_model.description: A workspace membership is an explicit record binding one operator to one workspace with a defined role. Operators are not automatically members of any workspace. Membership must be granted explicitly by a workspace owner or admin.
membership_model.planned_schema_entity: workspace_members table: id, workspace_id, operator_id, workspace_role, invited_by, joined_at, active, UNIQUE(workspace_id, operator_id)
invariants: Operator must belong to tenant before joining workspace
Cross-workspace resource lookups return 404 to prevent enumeration

ownership_model.description: Each workspace has exactly one owner_id. Resources carry both tenant_id and workspace_id. workspace_id is immutable after creation. Resources cannot be moved between workspaces.
workspace_ownership_rules: Each workspace has exactly one owner_id
Owner may transfer ownership to another active member with owner or admin role
Last owner cannot leave without transferring or deactivating workspace
resource_ownership_rules: Every business resource must carry both tenant_id and workspace_id after enforcement
workspace_id = NULL acceptable only in developer_preview and demo modes
Resources cannot be shared across workspace boundaries

workspace_level_roles.permissions: Full workspace control, membership management, ownership transfer

role_scope_model.composition_rule: Effective permission is the intersection of tenant-level role and workspace-level role. Workspace role cannot exceed tenant role ceiling.
authorization_check_order: 2. Does the operator belong to the resource tenant? (enforced)
3. Is the operator an active member of the resource workspace? (new)
4. Does the operator workspace role permit the requested operation? (new)

workspace_lifecycle_model.creation: POST /workspaces by tenant admin or owner role. Server assigns workspace_id, tenant_id, owner_id. Creating operator auto-added as workspace_owner member. workspace_created audit event emitted.
workspace_lifecycle_model.member_invitation: POST /workspaces/{id}/members by workspace owner/admin. Verifies inviting operator is active owner/admin. Verifies invited operator is in same tenant. workspace_member_added audit event emitted.
workspace_lifecycle_model.member_removal_leave: DELETE /workspaces/{id}/members/{operator_id} or POST /workspaces/{id}/leave. Last owner blocked from leaving. Membership set active=0. workspace_member_removed audit event emitted.
workspace_lifecycle_model.deactivation: POST /admin/workspaces/{id}/deactivate by tenant admin. Workspace active=0. All member access denied fail-closed. workspace_deactivated audit event emitted.
workspace_lifecycle_model.deletion: Deferred — audit immutability requires resource references remain accessible for audit review. Hard deletion requires separate data retention/erasure policy design.

tenant_admin_cannot: Read workspace-owned grant/request/execution resources unless also a workspace member
workspace_owner_can: Read/write all resources within workspace (subject to tenant-level role)
workspace_member_can: Read/write resources within their workspace per tenant+workspace role
admin_operator_ownership_boundary.invariant: Operator tenant_id and workspace membership are server-assigned and server-enforced. No client-supplied tenant_id or workspace_id value can override the server-derived workspace context.

derivation_order: 3a. Resolve workspace_id from X-Workspace-ID header if present and operator is active member
3b. Or use operator default workspace if single-workspace operator
3c. Or deny with 400 workspace_context_required if multi-workspace and no header
4. Verify operator is active member of resolved workspace
5. Propagate (tenant_id, workspace_id) to all resource reads/writes
injection_rules: All route handlers must receive (tenant_id, workspace_id) from server context
No route handler may accept workspace_id from request body for authorization
Only X-Workspace-ID header is trusted for workspace context selection
Data layer must validate workspace_id belongs to tenant_id before any operation

prevented_vectors: Request body workspace_id injection — ignored for authorization decisions
Request body tenant_id injection — always ignored
Non-workspace header tenant_id injection — always rejected
Token elevation — operator token carries tenant_id and operator_id only; workspace resolved at request time with membership verification
Cross-workspace resource address — query requires AND workspace_id = ? after enforcement
Null workspace bypass — workspace_id = NULL not a magic cross-workspace value; rejected in production-like modes
required_server_checks: 5. Filter all DB queries by (tenant_id, workspace_id)
6. Deny 403 if workspace membership check fails

read_route_filters: GET /grants: filter by tenant_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
GET /grants/{id}: verify tenant_id AND workspace_id match; return 404 otherwise
GET /grant-requests: filter by tenant_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
GET /audit-events: filter by tenant_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
GET /provenance: filter by tenant_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
cross_workspace_lookup_denial_model.response_code: Cross-workspace resource lookups return 404 (not 403) to prevent workspace enumeration
cross_workspace_lookup_denial_model.admin_exception: Tenant admin read via /admin/workspaces/{id}/resources; emits workspace_admin_access audit event

write_route_rules: POST /grants: workspace_id assigned from operator context; client-supplied workspace_id in body rejected
POST /grant-requests/{id}/approve: operator must be workspace member with workspace role permitting approval
POST /grants/{id}/revoke: must match tenant_id AND workspace_id of operator current context
cross_workspace_mutation_denial_model.mutation_denial_invariant: Any mutation that would assign workspace_id from client input or modify a resource whose workspace_id does not match the operator current workspace context must be denied fail-closed with 403 and a logged audit event.

audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.audit_event_propagation: Every AuditEvent must carry non-null workspace_id in production-like modes. workspace_id derived from operator context at event-write time. Null workspace_id in production-like mode rejected by audit writer.
audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.provenance_event_propagation: Every ProvenanceEvent must carry non-null workspace_id after enforcement. Provenance event workspace attribution must match resource workspace_id.
audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.evidence_archive: Evidence archive records must carry workspace_id. Evidence queried for a workspace filtered by workspace_id.
audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.hash_chain_impact: Audit hash chain (GL-103/104/105) must incorporate workspace_id as chain input field after enforcement. This is a breaking audit-chain change. Pre-enforcement records with workspace_id = null remain valid as legacy/developer-preview chain.
audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.compliance_gate: Future compliance-gate must verify all audit events in compliance window carry non-null workspace_id before compliance readiness is claimed.
audit_evidence_provenance_compliance_pro.retention_erasure: Data retention and erasure policies must be workspace-scoped. Deferred beyond GL-223.

columns: workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id)
operator_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES operators(id)

existing_table_changes: FK constraint from resource tables to workspaces(id) — deferred to implementation migration
Index on (tenant_id, workspace_id) for all resource tables — confirm existing coverage
workspace_role validation against allowlist: workspace_owner, workspace_admin, workspace_member, workspace_readonly
database_schema_plan.note: No schema changes are made in GL-223. All changes are planned for GL-224.

migration_backfill_plan.migration_id: 0012_gl224_workspace_enforcement (or next available number at implementation time)
steps: 3. Insert default/legacy workspace {id: default, tenant_id: demo, name: default}
4. Update resource records with workspace_id = NULL to workspace_id = default within tenant_id = demo
backfill_rules: Backfill only in developer_preview and demo runtime modes
Backfill must not touch records with non-null workspace_id
migration_backfill_plan.rollback: Drop workspace_members, drop workspaces, set workspace_id = NULL on backfilled resources
migration_backfill_plan.note: No migration is added in GL-223.

api_server_plan.modified_endpoints: All existing resource endpoints updated to accept X-Workspace-ID header, derive workspace_id server-side, filter by (tenant_id, workspace_id), reject unresolvable workspace context.
api_server_plan.auth_changes: check_auth() returns workspace_id in addition to tenant_id after GL-226. _get_workspace_context() helper added.
api_server_plan.backward_compatibility: Developer_preview and demo modes continue to function with null workspace during transition phases.
api_server_plan.note: No API changes are made in GL-223.

new_path_groups: /workspaces/* paths with schemas for WorkspaceCreateRequest, WorkspaceResponse, WorkspaceMemberAddRequest, WorkspaceMemberResponse, WorkspaceOwnershipTransferRequest
modified_components: All resource response schemas (GrantResponse, GrantRequestResponse, ExecutionResponse, AuditEventResponse) include non-nullable workspace_id after enforcement
X-Workspace-ID header documented as required on workspace-aware endpoints
operatorToken security scheme documented to require workspace membership verification
openapi_impact_plan.note: No OpenAPI changes are made in GL-223.

integration_tests: Full workspace lifecycle: create → add member → write grant → read grant → remove member → verify denied access
Cross-tenant workspace isolation: two tenants with same workspace name do not interfere
Audit event workspace propagation: all events in workspace have matching workspace_id
Backfill idempotency: running migration twice produces identical state
security_regression_tests: Body workspace_id injection does not change resource ownership
Token-derived workspace context cannot be overridden by request headers for tenant_id
gate_tests: All resource tables have non-null workspace_id in production-like mode
All audit events in compliance window have non-null workspace_id
No cross-workspace reads pass membership check

phase_3.title: Workspace context derivation and header support

rollout_strategy.note: Each phase is a separate commit/issue and can be reverted independently.

rollback_strategy.phase_1: Drop workspace tables, nullify backfilled workspace_id values
rollback_strategy.phase_4: Remove workspace filters from resource queries, revert to tenant-only filtering
rollback_strategy.phase_5: Revert audit writer to accept null workspace_id; hash chain break-point coordination required

demo_synthetic_compatibility.current_demo_state: Demo and synthetic flows use tenant_id = demo and workspace_id = NULL
demo_synthetic_compatibility.after_phase_1_backfill: Demo records will have workspace_id = default. Demo operator tokens assigned to default workspace via membership backfill.
demo_synthetic_compatibility.after_phase_3: X-Workspace-ID: default header will be required in demo mode if operator is member of multiple workspaces (auto-selected for single-workspace operators).
demo_synthetic_compatibility.required_update: grant_lifecycle_evidence_bundle.py example must be updated in implementation phase to send X-Workspace-ID if required.
demo_synthetic_compatibility.compatibility_verdict: All existing demo API smoke tests and quickstart flows must continue to pass through Phase 4 by targeting the default workspace.

production_readiness_impact.public_snapshot_preparation: CONDITIONAL, separate explicit gate required — unchanged
production_readiness_impact.note: GL-223 does not change any production-readiness tier.

controlled_preview_impact: GL-223 does not change the Controlled External Technical Review posture. Review materials remain synthetic/demo only.
real_data_impact: GL-223 does not enable real customer, private grant, or institutional data. These tiers remain NO-GO.

remaining_blockers.description: workspace_id not enforced on any resource routes
No workspace membership verification in auth/route layer

proposed_implementation_issue_breakdown.title: Workspace Schema Migration and Entity Layer
Workspace CRUD API and Admin Control Plane
Workspace Context Derivation and Header Support
Workspace Audit Event and Provenance Enforcement

risk_register.mitigation: Idempotent backfill with transaction rollback; test in demo mode before schema merge

risk_register.risk: Workspace enforcement breaks existing demo/quickstart flows
risk_register.mitigation: Backwards-compatible phase approach; demo mode auto-selects default workspace

risk_register.risk: Audit hash chain incompatibility after workspace_id addition
risk_register.mitigation: Treat pre-enforcement chain as legacy; start new chain segment at enforcement boundary

risk_register.risk: Workspace owner cannot leave — operational dead-end
risk_register.mitigation: Ownership transfer flow required before leave; admin break-glass documented
Membership verification gates all workspace context claims before trust
Null-workspace rejection gate in production-like mode; gate script verification

risk_register.risk: Migration rollback complexity increases with phases
risk_register.mitigation: Each phase independently reversible; rollback procedures documented per phase

findings.finding: workspace_id column exists on all resource tables but is null for all existing records
No workspaces or workspace_members tables exist
No workspace membership verification in any route handler
All cross-tenant lookup denial is enforced; cross-workspace denial is not
Audit events carry workspace_id = null throughout
Operator model is tenant-aware but not workspace-aware
Server-derived tenant_id model is sound and can be extended to workspace
The phased rollout plan preserves all existing GL-214 through GL-222 boundaries
Demo/synthetic flows are compatible with the planned default-workspace backfill
The audit hash chain will require a defined break-point at workspace enforcement boundary
